title: Tool Range Day
prerequisites: Kali Linux with all default tools
learning_objectives: Verify and operate essential Kali Linux security tools
Compare tool capabilities and outputs against the same target
Select appropriate tools for different engagement scenarios
scenario: A proper operator knows their tools. Set up your Kali environment and verify every essential tool works. Then use 3 different tools against the lab target to understand their distinct capabilities and when to choose each one.

scenario_context.setting: Kali Linux tool validation and comparison
scenario_context.challenge: Master tool selection and understand capability differences
scenario_context.relevance: Using the wrong tool wastes time and may miss findings. Understanding each tool's strengths is essential for effective engagements.

subtasks.title: Environment Verification
subtasks.description: Verify all tools from the checklist are installed and working (Nmap, Burp Suite, Wireshark, Python3, Netcat, Nikto, Gobuster).
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Run each tool's version/help command. Verify it launches and basic functions work. Screenshot each verification.
subtasks.evidence_required: Tool verification screenshots for all 7 tools

subtasks.title: Nmap vs Nikto Comparison
subtasks.description: Run both against the lab web server. Compare what each discovers and their different approaches.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Nmap focuses on ports and services; Nikto focuses on web vulnerabilities and misconfigurations. Run both. Compare findings. Note what each catches that the other misses.
subtasks.evidence_required: Both tool outputs plus comparison report

subtasks.title: Wireshark Capture
subtasks.description: Capture traffic while running one scan. Identify scan patterns in the packet capture.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Start Wireshark capture. Run an Nmap scan. Stop capture. Analyse: what does a scan look like at the packet level? Identify SYN patterns, response patterns.
subtasks.evidence_required: PCAP file plus analysis of scan traffic patterns

subtasks.title: Tool Selection Report
subtasks.description: Given 5 scenarios (web app, network, wireless, password, forensics), recommend which tools to use and justify.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: For each scenario: primary tool, secondary tool, justification. Consider: what the tool does, what it misses, when it's inappropriate. Be specific.

reflection_prompt: A new tool gets released that claims to replace Nmap, Nikto, and Gobuster all in one. Would you adopt it immediately? What criteria would you use to evaluate a new tool?

quiz.question: Nikto differs from Nmap in that it specifically:
options: Tests for known web server vulnerabilities and misconfigurations
quiz.explanation: Nikto is a web server scanner that checks for thousands of known vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, and interesting files — something Nmap doesn't do.

quiz.question: Capturing scan traffic with Wireshark helps you understand:
options: What your scans look like to defenders and IDS systems
quiz.explanation: Understanding how your tools look on the wire helps you anticipate detection and adjust your approach on real engagements.

mentor_notes: All 7 tools must be verified. The comparison and scenario reasoning are where students demonstrate real understanding versus just running commands.
